mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.5% signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending power, which forces S&P 500 earnings down and produces negative forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with an out-of-sample R-squared above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production reflects contracting manufacturing output and supply chain bottlenecks, triggering short-term risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of months where INDPRO declines during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid inflation acceleration above 0.4% monthly forces the Federal Reserve to anticipate aggressive rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are non-negative in more than 50% of periods following CPIAUCSL monthly spikes above 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate profit margins, creating a persistent upward drift in S&P 500 prices over a 60-day forward horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS growth against 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal consumption expenditures signals weakening household demand relative to overall output, forecasting corporate earnings misses and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS growth rates shows no predictive power for 20-day SPX direction in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates resilient supply-side capacity that offsets rate hike headwinds, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns are negative in more than 65% of instances where INDPRO rises during tightening cycles.
